task: Write a bash script that backs up a PostgreSQL database to S3 with rotation, logging, and error notifications

criteria.description: Script performs correct pg_dump → compress → upload → rotate pipeline
criteria.pass_condition: Uses pg_dump with appropriate flags. Compresses output (gzip/zstd). Uploads to S3 with aws cli. Rotates old backups by age or count.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if pg_dump with appropriate format flags, 0.5 if basic, 0.0 if wrong
1.0 if compressed (gzip/zstd), 0.0 if raw SQL upload

sub_attributes.description: aws s3 cp/sync with correct path and options
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if correct aws s3 cp with proper path, 0.0 if wrong

sub_attributes.description: Deletes backups older than N days or keeps last N
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implemented correctly, 0.5 if partially, 0.0 if missing

pass_examples: pg_dump -Fc | gzip > backup.gz && aws s3 cp ... && find/delete old
fail_examples: pg_dump with no compression, no rotation logic

criteria.description: Script is safe — set -euo pipefail, no secrets in code, cleanup on failure
criteria.pass_condition: set -euo pipefail. Trap for cleanup. No hardcoded passwords. Uses .pgpass or env vars. Temp files cleaned up.
pass_examples: set -euo pipefail, trap cleanup EXIT, reads creds from env
fail_examples: No error handling, password in script, temp files left behind

criteria.description: Comprehensive logging with timestamps and log levels
criteria.pass_condition: Timestamped log function. Logs start/success/failure/rotation. Writes to file AND stdout. Includes backup size and duration.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if log() function with ISO timestamps, 0.5 if echo with date, 0.0 if plain echo

sub_attributes.description: Logs all key events (start, size, duration, rotate, done)
Writes to both file and stdout
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if tee to file + stdout, 0.5 if one, 0.0 if neither

fail_examples: Random echo statements with no timestamps

criteria.description: Error notifications via practical channel (email, Slack, PagerDuty)
criteria.pass_condition: On failure: sends notification with error details and context. Configurable notification channel. Includes backup name, error message, timestamp.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implemented (curl to Slack, mail, etc.), 0.0 if TODO/placeholder

sub_attributes.description: Notification includes useful context (timestamp, db, error)
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if rich context, 0.5 if basic, 0.0 if just 'backup failed'
1.0 if configurable, 0.5 if hardcoded but works, 0.0 if neither

pass_examples: Slack webhook with formatted message including db name, error, and log tail

criteria.description: Script is configurable via environment variables with sensible defaults
criteria.pass_condition: Key params via env vars: DB_NAME, S3_BUCKET, RETENTION_DAYS, etc. Defaults provided. Usage/help flag. Validates required vars.

sub_attributes.description: Key params from env vars with defaults
sub_attributes.measurement: % of configurable params using env vars with fallback defaults

sub_attributes.description: Validates required vars exist before starting
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if checks all required vars, 0.5 if some, 0.0 if none

fail_examples: Hardcoded db name, bucket, and retention in script body
